# groundcover OTEL CI/CD Export Action

This repository contains a GitHub Action that exports GitHub Actions workflow runs as OpenTelemetry traces.

## Canonical files

- `action.yml`: canonical input and output contract
- `README.md`: usage, permissions, examples, and operational guidance
- `GITHUB_ACTION_SPEC.md`: full implementation requirements
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: contributor workflow and verification requirements

## Groundcover usage rules

- Use a workspace-specific OTLP endpoint provided by groundcover.
- Do not hardcode `https://otel.groundcover.com/v1/traces`.
- Pass the endpoint through `otlpEndpoint`.
- Authenticate with a `Third Party` ingestion key using `otlpHeaders: "apikey=..."`.
- See `https://docs.groundcover.com/integrations/data-sources/opentelemetry/sending-from-an-opentelemetry-collector` for endpoint setup.
- See `https://docs.groundcover.com/use-groundcover/remote-access-and-apis/ingestion-keys` for ingestion key creation.
- Do not use `Authorization: Bearer ...` for OTLP ingest. That header format is for groundcover REST API usage.

## Supported behavior

- Export workflow runs, jobs, and steps as nested spans.
- Support `traceparent` to link CI/CD traces with application traces.
- Support same-workflow export and separate `workflow_run` export.
- For separate workflow trace linking, persist the `traceparent` in the source workflow and pass it into the export workflow.
- Always set resource attribute `source=github-actions`.
- Support configurable `workload` and optional `env` resource attributes.

## Implementation rules

- Keep the runtime on Node 20.
- Keep TypeScript strict mode enabled.
- Never log secrets.
- Mask sensitive OTLP headers with `core.setSecret()`.
- Preserve committed bundle output in `dist/`.
- After changes, run `npm run all`.

## Useful paths

- `src/runner.ts`: action orchestration
- `src/tracer.ts`: OTLP exporter setup and `traceparent` extraction
- `src/trace/workflow.ts`: workflow root span generation
- `src/trace/job.ts`: job span generation
- `src/trace/step.ts`: step span generation
